info.name: Nguyen
info.title: M.Sc. Interaction Technology
info.welcometext:
I am passionate about researching how technology can benefit humans and society, in particular the field of responsible and trustworthy AI.
                    I believe that AI offers a great potential to improve our lives and contribute to solving issues but the implementation must ensure fairness and transparency.

education.title: M.Sc. Interaction Technology
education.description:
A 2-year master's degree that focuses on the human and technology, with courses in machine learning, deep learning, research methods, design and ethics. I decided to focus my study on applied machine learning and graduated with distinction with a thesis in the field of explainable AI for spiking neural networks under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Christin Seifert, Dr. ing. Gwenn Englebienne and Meike Nauta, M.Sc. 
     During my studies, I also worked as TA in NLP and Data Science (grading homework, supervising tutorials).

education.title: B.Sc. Computer Science and Management
education.description:
A dual 7-semester bachelor's degree in cooperation with Airbus, with courses in IT project management and organization, software engineering, programming, theoretical and technical foundations of computer science, business administration and finance. I graduated with a thesis in requirements development for PLM systems in the aviation industry in an Industry 4.0 setting under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Hinrich Schröder and Alcibiades Gonzalez-Noval (Airbus). 
 I was also working as a TA for Discrete Mathematics 1 and 2 during my studies, and spent an Erasmus semester at the ebs Paris with a focus on intercultural management.

work.title: Student Research Assistant XAI
work.description: Part of a research team across the universities of Twente and Duisburg-Essen that performed a literature study on evaluation methods for XAI led by Meike Nauta.

work.title: IT Project Manager
work.description: Scrum Master for a CAD software legacy retirement project as well as project support in risk, budget and stakeholder management in the PLM area. Additionally, I also had tasks in the technical and financial supplier bundle management of my department. On top, I was a change agent in an organizational transformation program towards an agile organization structure (SAFe).

work.title: Dual Student in Computer Science and Management
work.description: As a dual student I switched in between university and internships at the ICT department at Airbus in Hamburg. In this time, I absolved internships in IT governance, project- and service management, with one internship in Toulouse, France. I was also part of Airbus' vocational training IT team, who took over some administrative IT tasks.

volunteer.title: Tutor
volunteer.description: Helping a third-grader with homework twice a week.

volunteer.title: Tutor
volunteer.description: Tutoring students in secondary school in Germany, including the preparation for the Abitur in Maths and English.

portfolio.name: Temporal Spike Attribution
portfolio.keywords: Explainable AI, spiking neural networks, feature attribution
portfolio.description: Development and evaluation of a local, post-hoc heatmap-type explanation for a prediction of temporally coded spiking neural networks using the model internal variables. This method is demonstrated on a time-series classification task.

portfolio.name: Automated Summarization of Breast Cancer Radiology Reports
portfolio.keywords: Abstractive summarization, medical data science, encoder-decoder with attention
portfolio.description: Automated summarization of Dutch Breast Cancer Radiology reports using an Encoder-Decoder model with Attention and a text classifier (SVM) for extracting the correct cancer severity score (BI-RADS).

portfolio.name: Pokérator - Unveil your inner Pokémon
portfolio.description: Creative naming and language generation based on user input using syllable-based and character-based n-gram models as well as OpenAI's GPT-2.

portfolio.name: Defending against Projected Gradient Descent
portfolio.keywords: Adversarial attack, data augmentation, deep learning
portfolio.description: Testing generalization through data augmentation, adversarial training, and combinations as a defense against adversarial attacks with the PGD attack. This project was developed in the frame of the course Deep Learning at the University of Twente.

portfolio.name: Sentiment Classifier from Speech
portfolio.description: Binary decision tree classifier for stressed speech features (MFCC, pitch, pitch energy) based on self-recorded data of 5 participants. This project was developed in the frame of the course Speech Processing at the University of Twente.
